# AI Layoff Tracker — LLM Guide

## Overview
A public research database tracking documented workforce reductions where AI or automation was explicitly cited. 65,117 jobs documented across 15 companies (as of v1.0). 4-tier classification system.

## Key URLs
- Homepage: https://damgeed.github.io/ai-layoff-tracker/
- Methodology: https://damgeed.github.io/ai-layoff-tracker/methodology.html
- Data Dictionary: https://damgeed.github.io/ai-layoff-tracker/data-dictionary.html
- Corrections: https://damgeed.github.io/ai-layoff-tracker/corrections.html
- Changelog: https://damgeed.github.io/ai-layoff-tracker/changelog.html

## API Endpoints
- Full dataset: /api/entries.json
- Statistics: /api/stats.json
- CSV export: /api/entries.csv
- RSS feed: /api/feed.xml
- Per-company: /api/company/{slug}.json (15 endpoints)
- Per-industry: /api/industry/{slug}.json (6 endpoints)
- Per-country: /api/country/{slug}.json (3 endpoints)
- Source citations: /api/sources.json
- Changelog: /api/changelog.json
- Corrections schema: /api/corrections-schema.json

## Classification System
- Tier 1: DIRECT_AI_REPLACEMENT — Company explicitly says AI replaced work
- Tier 2: AI_DRIVEN_RESTRUCTURING — Company cites AI as major factor
- Tier 3: AI_REALLOCATION — Jobs cut while redirecting resources to AI
- Tier 4: MARKET_DISRUPTION — Revenue loss due to AI competition

## Data Fields
See /data-dictionary.html for complete field reference.

## GitHub
https://github.com/Damgeed/ai-layoff-tracker
